case_title: STEVEN FUNK VS. ELIZABETH COLLET FUNK et al

docket_entries.description: LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELIZABETH COLLET FUNK'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED: MOTION TO DISMISS IS GRANTED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION TO STAY IS OFF CALENDAR. THE ALBERTA COURT IS A SUITABLE FORUM. JUDICIAL ECONOMY, THE PUBLIC INTEREST, AND THE PRIVATE INTERESTS OF THE PARTIES MANDATE THAT ALL OF THE PARTIES' FINANCIAL DISPUTES BE DECIDED IN A SINGLE FORUM. THUS, BECAUSE OF THE PENDENCY OF THE ACTION IN ALBERTA, THIS COURT IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM. MOREOVER, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SUCCEEDED IN HIS MOTION IN THE DIVORCE CASE BASED ON THE ARGUMENT THAT "CALIFORNIA IS NOT THE APPROPRIATE FORUM FOR RESOLUTION OF THE FINANCIAL ISSUES IN THIS DIVORCE," PLAINTIFF IS JUDICIALLY ESTOPPED TO OPPOSE THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT WAIVED STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-04-2011 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-28-2011 Off Calendar

MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action
Off Calendar Nov-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ELIZABETH COLLET FUNK'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION.

APPEARANCES: David B. Tillotson, Esq. of Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick LLP 957-1800 for pltf & opposing party; Gordon C. Atkinson, Esq. 693-2088 & Abigail E. Pringle, Esq. 693-2169 of Cooley LLP for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: Motion to dismiss is granted and the alternate motion to stay is off calendar. The Alberta court is a suitable forum. Judicial economy, the public interest, and the private interests of the parties mandate that all of the parties' financial disputes be decided in a single forum. Thus, because of the pendency of the action in Alberta, this court is a seriously inconvenient forum. Moreover, because plaintiff succeeded in his motion in the divorce case based on the argument that "California is not the appropriate forum for resolution of the financial issues in this divorce," plaintiff is judicially estopped to oppose this motion. Defendant waived statute of limitations. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353
